title: 95-lesson milestone
description: Five lessons from the finish — checkpoint at 97 WPM
text: Five lessons remain. You have already done the hard work. The fingers know the keys; the eyes read ahead; the mind no longer thinks in letters but in phrases and paragraphs. The remaining lessons exist not to add new skill, but to confirm what you have already built — and to give you a number to be proud of for the rest of your career.

tips.text: Five lessons left. The hard part is behind you; this stretch is the proof.
97 words a minute is quicker than most people talk. You write faster than you speak now.

tips.target: Five lessons remain. You have already done the hard work. The fingers know the keys; the eyes read ahead; the mind no longer thinks in letters but in phrases and paragraphs. The

tips.text: The last page is lesson 99: everything at once, 105 words a minute. You'll be ready.

tips.target: remaining lessons exist not to add new skill, but to confirm what you have already built — and to give you a number to be proud of for the rest of your career.

character_tips.anna: Five to go. What's left isn't more work to learn, it's the same skill, confirmed.
character_tips.maxim: 97 a minute, faster than most people speak. The finish is in sight. Keep your head down.
character_tips.knopych: Five left. This part is the steady walk home, not the climb.
character_tips.klavochka: Five short steps to the top. You've earned the view; don't rush the last of it.
